units.description: Range bound: the result is in [0, min(len(a), len(b))]. (LCS length (variant 7): emphasize quadratic-time witness structure without requiring explicit backpointer extraction.)
common_failure_modes: stating only an upper bound of min(len(a), len(b))

units.description: Existential witness: there exist strictly increasing index sequences into a and b of equal length k pointing to equal values. (LCS length (variant 7): emphasize quadratic-time witness structure without requiring explicit backpointer extraction.)
common_failure_modes: omitting the strict-increase condition on indices
comparing values element-wise across slices of different lengths

units.description: Maximality: no common subsequence strictly longer than the returned length exists. (LCS length (variant 7): emphasize quadratic-time witness structure without requiring explicit backpointer extraction.)
common_failure_modes: confusing maximality with uniqueness of witness

units.description: Contiguous vs subsequence distinction: the definition allows non-contiguous index selections. (LCS length (variant 7): emphasize quadratic-time witness structure without requiring explicit backpointer extraction.)
common_failure_modes: solving the longest common substring instead of subsequence

units.description: Symmetry: lcsLength a b = lcsLength b a. (LCS length (variant 7): emphasize quadratic-time witness structure without requiring explicit backpointer extraction.)
